tags.name: oauth
tags.description: OAuth-adjacent public flows used by the reference implementation

tags.name: grants
tags.description: Grant initiation, approval, revocation, and introspection

tags.name: event-subscriptions
tags.description: Client event-subscription management (RI extension; CloudEvents 1.0 + Standard Webhooks delivery)

get.summary: Unauthenticated cold-start pointer at the resource server root. Names the well-known endpoint, the `/v1/schema` capability discovery surface, the core query base, and the running reference revision so a probe learns the next hop without trial-and-error.
Return RFC 8414 authorization-server metadata with the reference provider-connect capability extensions.
Return RFC 9728 protected-resource metadata advertising the PDPP query base and owner-self-export capabilities.
Return RFC 9728 protected-resource metadata for the hosted MCP endpoint.

post.summary: Register a public client through the reference dynamic client registration profile.

401.description: Missing or invalid initial access token

post.summary: Stage a PDPP data-access request and receive a pending-consent request_uri plus authorization URL.

403.description: Request rejected because the resolved grant contract is invalid

connection_id.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.

post.summary: Approve a pending data-access request through the JSON consent surface used by tests and automation.

200.description: Grant approved and client token issued

connection_id.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.

403.description: Grant is malformed or no longer valid

post.summary: Redeem a short-lived single-use consent exchange code from the hosted HTML consent flow for the client token.

200.description: Exchange code redeemed and client token issued

connection_id.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.

410.description: Exchange code expired or already redeemed

post.summary: Start the owner device flow used for owner-self-export and dashboard bootstrap.
Exchange an OAuth device code, authorization code, or refresh token for a bearer token.

500.description: Server error while exchanging the device code

post.summary: Inspect token activity and, for active client tokens, the bound grant projection.

connection_id.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.

post.summary: Revoke a grant and all tokens minted from it.

403.description: Grant is malformed or no longer valid

get.summary: List connector or source boundaries visible under the bearer token, with stream summaries and coarse capability hints.

403.description: Grant does not permit this request

get.summary: Return the caller-visible source/stream capability graph in one shot. Owner tokens see every owner-visible connector; client tokens see only the grant's source and streams. Each stream entry reuses the per-stream metadata shape (schema, query declarations, field capabilities, expand capabilities, freshness).

granted_connections.description: Connections the caller's grant authorizes for this stream under the addressed connector. Clients MAY pass any `connection_id` here on a subsequent read to scope without trial-and-error. Omitted for provider-native sources where connection identity does not apply.

connection_id.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.

display_name.description: Owner-meaningful label for the connection. Never the storage-layer placeholder (`legacy`, `default_account`); falls back to `<connector> · account N` when the owner has not renamed the connection.

403.description: Grant does not permit this request

get.summary: List streams available under the current grant or owner scope. Returns stream-level totals only; for per-field filter capabilities (exact, range operators, aggregation) call `GET /v1/schema` first and consult `field_capabilities` per stream before issuing `filter[...]` queries on `/v1/streams/{stream}/records`. Multi-connection deployments emit one entry per (stream, connection_id); each entry carries `connection_id` and a `display_name` so callers can attribute and disambiguate.

schema.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.
Deprecated wire alias for `connection_id`. Emitted alongside `connection_id` during the migration window. New clients SHOULD ignore this field and read `connection_id` instead.

connection_id.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.

display_name.description: Owner-meaningful label for the connection. Never the storage-layer placeholder (`legacy`, `default_account`); falls back to `<connector> · account N` when the owner has not renamed the connection.

connector_instance_id.description: Deprecated wire alias for `connection_id`. Emitted alongside `connection_id` during the migration window. New clients SHOULD ignore this field and read `connection_id` instead.

403.description: Grant does not permit this request

get.summary: Return stream metadata including declared query capabilities and advisory freshness. For per-field filter capabilities on this stream (exact, range operators, aggregation), prefer `GET /v1/schema` first and read `field_capabilities` rather than guessing `filter[...]` shapes against the records endpoint. Pass `connection_id` (or the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias) to restrict to a single connection; omitted, the response aggregates across the connections the grant authorizes.

schema.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.
Deprecated wire alias for `connection_id`. Emitted alongside `connection_id` during the migration window. New clients SHOULD ignore this field and read `connection_id` instead.

granted_connections.description: Connections the caller's grant authorizes for this stream under the addressed connector. Clients MAY pass any `connection_id` here on a subsequent read to scope without trial-and-error. Omitted for provider-native sources where connection identity does not apply.

connection_id.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.

display_name.description: Owner-meaningful label for the connection. Never the storage-layer placeholder (`legacy`, `default_account`); falls back to `<connector> · account N` when the owner has not renamed the connection.

403.description: Grant does not permit this request

get.summary: List records in a stream under grant enforcement. Supports logical-cursor pagination, exact and declared range filters, declared one-hop expansion, and changes_since. Per-field filter operators, sortable fields, expandable relations, projection, search modes, and count support are advertised by `GET /v1/schema` (`field_capabilities`, `expand_capabilities`); consult it before issuing `filter[...]`, `expand[]`, or `fields=` shapes to avoid 400 errors. Pass `connection_id` to restrict to one connection; the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias is accepted for compatibility but new clients SHOULD use `connection_id`.

schema.description: Opaque logical pagination cursor. Encodes (cursor_field, primary_key) position.
`beginning` for initial sync, or an opaque changes-since token from next_changes_since. Distinct from list-page cursors.
Per-field filter map. Exact: `filter[field]=value`. Range: `filter[field][op]=value` where `op` is one of the declared `field_capabilities.range_filter.operators` from `GET /v1/schema`.
Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.
Deprecated wire alias for `connection_id`. Emitted alongside `connection_id` during the migration window. New clients SHOULD ignore this field and read `connection_id` instead.

connection_id.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.

display_name.description: Owner-meaningful label for the connection. Never the storage-layer placeholder (`legacy`, `default_account`); falls back to `<connector> · account N` when the owner has not renamed the connection.

connector_instance_id.description: Deprecated wire alias for `connection_id`. Emitted alongside `connection_id` during the migration window. New clients SHOULD ignore this field and read `connection_id` instead.

403.description: Grant does not permit this request

get.summary: Compute a single-stream grant-safe aggregation. Supports count, numeric sum, numeric/date min/max, exact count_distinct, scalar grouped counts (`group_by`), calendar time-bucket counts (`group_by_time`+`granularity`, optional `time_zone` defaulting to UTC), and existing exact/range filters over declared fields. Exactly one grouping dimension per call: `group_by` XOR `group_by_time`.

schema.description: Group counts into calendar time buckets over a declared date/date-time field. Mutually exclusive with `group_by`. Requires `granularity`.
Calendar `date_trunc` unit for `group_by_time`. Required when `group_by_time` is present and forbidden otherwise.
IANA time zone used to compute `group_by_time` bucket boundaries. Defaults to `UTC`; the response echoes the effective zone.
Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.
Deprecated wire alias for `connection_id`. Emitted alongside `connection_id` during the migration window. New clients SHOULD ignore this field and read `connection_id` instead.

403.description: Grant does not permit this request

get.summary: Fetch a single record by primary key under grant enforcement, with optional declared one-hop expansion. Expandable relations and the per-relation `expand_limit` ceiling are advertised by `GET /v1/schema` (`expand_capabilities`); requesting an unadvertised relation is rejected rather than silently ignored. When the identifier resolves to more than one connection under the caller's grant and `connection_id` is omitted, returns a typed `ambiguous_connection` (409) error with `available_connections` and retry guidance instead of silently picking one. The deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias is accepted for compatibility but new clients SHOULD use `connection_id`.

schema.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.
Deprecated wire alias for `connection_id`. Emitted alongside `connection_id` during the migration window. New clients SHOULD ignore this field and read `connection_id` instead.

connection_id.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.

display_name.description: Owner-meaningful label for the connection. Never the storage-layer placeholder (`legacy`, `default_account`); falls back to `<connector> · account N` when the owner has not renamed the connection.

connector_instance_id.description: Deprecated wire alias for `connection_id`. Emitted alongside `connection_id` during the migration window. New clients SHOULD ignore this field and read `connection_id` instead.

403.description: Grant does not permit this request

409.description: Identifier resolves to more than one connection under the caller's grant. Retry with the `connection_id` listed in `error.available_connections`.

connection_id.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.

display_name.description: Owner-meaningful label for the connection. Never the storage-layer placeholder (`legacy`, `default_account`); falls back to `<connector> · account N` when the owner has not renamed the connection.

get.summary: Optional lexical retrieval extension: search records across authorized streams by text. Search modes, per-mode cursor support, and field-level `lexical_search`/`semantic_search` capabilities are advertised by `GET /v1/schema`; `filter[...]` operators applied to a single named stream must come from that stream's `field_capabilities`. Hits carry `connection_id` for attribution; the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias is emitted alongside for compatibility but new clients SHOULD read `connection_id`.

schema.description: Opaque logical pagination cursor. Encodes (cursor_field, primary_key) position.
Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.
Deprecated wire alias for `connection_id`. Emitted alongside `connection_id` during the migration window. New clients SHOULD ignore this field and read `connection_id` instead.

connection_id.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.

display_name.description: Owner-meaningful label for the connection. Never the storage-layer placeholder (`legacy`, `default_account`); falls back to `<connector> · account N` when the owner has not renamed the connection.

connector_instance_id.description: Deprecated wire alias for `connection_id`. Emitted alongside `connection_id` during the migration window. New clients SHOULD ignore this field and read `connection_id` instead.

400.description: Invalid request (e.g. unsupported v1 query parameter, missing q)

403.description: Grant does not permit a named stream (client tokens only)

410.description: Cursor expired or refers to an unknown snapshot

get.summary: Experimental optional extension: semantic retrieval across authorized streams by text. See the semantic-retrieval capability spec. Unstable in v1. Per-stream semantic capability and pagination support are advertised by `GET /v1/schema` and the `capabilities.semantic_retrieval` block in protected-resource metadata; consult them before relying on cursors or filters. Hits carry `connection_id` for attribution; the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias is emitted for compatibility only.

schema.description: Opaque logical pagination cursor. Encodes (cursor_field, primary_key) position.
Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.
Deprecated wire alias for `connection_id`. Emitted alongside `connection_id` during the migration window. New clients SHOULD ignore this field and read `connection_id` instead.

display_name.description: Owner-meaningful label for the connection. Never the storage-layer placeholder (`legacy`, `default_account`); falls back to `<connector> · account N` when the owner has not renamed the connection.

connector_instance_id.description: Deprecated wire alias for `connection_id`. Emitted alongside `connection_id` during the migration window. New clients SHOULD ignore this field and read `connection_id` instead.

400.description: Invalid request (e.g. unsupported v1 query parameter, missing q)

403.description: Grant does not permit a named stream (client tokens only)

410.description: Cursor expired or refers to an unknown snapshot

get.summary: Experimental optional extension: hybrid retrieval blending lexical and semantic recall under one grant-safe result list. See the hybrid-retrieval capability spec. Hybrid does NOT support cursor pagination on this reference; check `pdpp_discovery_hints.hybrid_pagination_supported` in the protected-resource metadata and, when it is `false` or absent, fall back to `GET /v1/search` (lexical) which supports `cursor`.

schema.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.
Deprecated wire alias for `connection_id`. Emitted alongside `connection_id` during the migration window. New clients SHOULD ignore this field and read `connection_id` instead.

display_name.description: Owner-meaningful label for the connection. Never the storage-layer placeholder (`legacy`, `default_account`); falls back to `<connector> · account N` when the owner has not renamed the connection.

connector_instance_id.description: Deprecated wire alias for `connection_id`. Emitted alongside `connection_id` during the migration window. New clients SHOULD ignore this field and read `connection_id` instead.

400.description: Invalid request (e.g. unsupported v1 query parameter, missing q, cursor parameter)

403.description: Grant does not permit a named stream (client tokens only)

404.description: Hybrid retrieval not advertised on this server

post.summary: Upload connector/runtime-owned blob bytes for a bound record.

200.description: Canonical content-addressed blob identity for the uploaded bytes

403.description: Owner/runtime authority required

get.summary: Fetch blob bytes authorized by the caller having discovered the referencing record under grant. When the blob identifier resolves to more than one connection under the caller's grant and `connection_id` is omitted, returns a typed `ambiguous_connection` (409) error with `available_connections` and retry guidance instead of silently picking one. The deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias is accepted for compatibility but new clients SHOULD use `connection_id`.

schema.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.
Deprecated wire alias for `connection_id`. Emitted alongside `connection_id` during the migration window. New clients SHOULD ignore this field and read `connection_id` instead.

403.description: Grant does not permit this request

409.description: Identifier resolves to more than one connection under the caller's grant. Retry with the `connection_id` listed in `error.available_connections`.

connection_id.description: Canonical public identifier for a connection (one owner-configured account/device/profile). Prefer this over the deprecated `connector_instance_id` alias.

display_name.description: Owner-meaningful label for the connection. Never the storage-layer placeholder (`legacy`, `default_account`); falls back to `<connector> · account N` when the owner has not renamed the connection.

post.summary: Create a client event subscription. Immediately enqueues a `pdpp.subscription.verify` event to the callback URL. The subscription stays in `pending_verification` until the receiver echoes the `challenge` value. Returns the per-subscription HMAC signing secret (`whsec_*`) once; it cannot be retrieved again.

callback_url.description: HTTPS endpoint that will receive CloudEvents 1.0 structured-mode JSON POST requests signed with Standard Webhooks headers. `http://localhost` is accepted for development.

streams.description: Subset of grant-scoped stream names to subscribe to. Omit to subscribe to all streams in the grant.

201.description: Subscription created. The `secret` field is the Standard Webhooks signing key (`whsec_<base64>`) and is returned only on creation.

secret.description: Standard Webhooks HMAC signing secret (`whsec_<base64>`). Store securely; returned only on creation and on secret rotation.

400.description: Invalid request (callback URL malformed, filters not in grant, etc.)

403.description: Bearer token is authenticated but is not a client token for an active grant.

get.summary: List all non-deleted event subscriptions for the bearer's `(client_id, grant_id)` pair.

403.description: Bearer token is authenticated but is not a client token for an active grant.

get.summary: Get a single event subscription owned by the bearer.

403.description: Bearer token is authenticated but is not a client token for an active grant.

404.description: Subscription not found or not owned by the bearer

patch.summary: Update an event subscription. Toggle `enabled` to disable or re-enable delivery. Set `rotate_secret` to true to generate a new signing secret (returned in the response body; old secret is immediately invalid).

enabled.description: Set to `false` to disable delivery; `true` to re-enable a `disabled` or `disabled_failure` subscription. Cannot re-enable a `disabled_revoked` subscription.

rotate_secret.description: Generate a new `whsec_*` signing secret. The new secret is returned in the response body. The old secret is immediately invalid.

200.description: Updated subscription. `secret` is only present when `rotate_secret` was `true`.

secret.description: New Standard Webhooks signing secret (`whsec_<base64>`). Present only when `rotate_secret` was `true`.

400.description: Invalid update (e.g. re-enabling a revoked subscription)

403.description: Bearer token is authenticated but is not a client token for an active grant.

404.description: Subscription not found or not owned by the bearer

delete.summary: Delete an event subscription. Queued undelivered events are dropped. Idempotent for the caller's `(client_id, grant_id)` pair.

403.description: Bearer token is authenticated but is not a client token for an active grant.

404.description: Subscription not found or not owned by the bearer

post.summary: Enqueue a `pdpp.subscription.test` event for asynchronous delivery to the subscription's callback URL. Accepted for `active` and `pending_verification` subscriptions. Returns the enqueued event ID.

403.description: Bearer token is authenticated but is not a client token for an active grant.

404.description: Subscription not found or not owned by the bearer

409.description: Subscription is not in a state that accepts test events (must be `active` or `pending_verification`)
